title: The Day is Dead.
content:
The day is dead,
    And evening trails her purple robes
        In fading fires of red.

        The day is dead.
    And yonder lily welcomes sleep
        And nods her weary head.

        The day is dead,
    And night droops low her sable plumes
        To mourn the glory fled.
meaning:
Fannie Isabelle Sherrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Day is Dead."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
